Definition (EN): A case definition is a kind of calculation definition (see 7.19 ) that produces a result intended to achieve a specific objective regarding a given subject.
Context/Example (EN): A case is a general concept that may be used in its own right, but also provides the basis for more specific kinds of cases, including analysis cases (see 7.23 ), verification cases (see 7.24 ), and use cases (see 7.25 ).

Definition (EN): A case usage is a kind of calculation usage that is a usage of a case definition.
Context/Example (EN): A case is a general concept that may be used in its own right, but also provides the basis for more specific kinds of cases, including analysis cases (see 7.23 ), verification cases (see 7.24 ), and use cases (see 7.25 ).

Definition (EN): The subject of a case is modeled as a distinguished parameter, similarly to the subject of a requirement (see 7.21 ).
Context/Example (EN): case def FaultRecovery { subject system : AutomationSystem; actor engineer : Person; objective { doc /* The engineer determines the cause of the system fault and resolves it returning the system to nominal operation. */ } }

Definition (EN): The objective of a case is modeled as a requirement usage to be satisfied by the performance of the case.
Context/Example (EN): case def FaultRecovery { subject system : AutomationSystem; actor engineer : Person; objective { doc /* The engineer determines the cause of the system fault and resolves it returning the system to nominal operation. */ } }

Equivalent (JP): (ケースの)アクターパラメータ
Definition (EN): A case definition or usage may also have one or more actor parameters that represent roles played by an entity external to the subject of the case but necessary to the specification of the case.
Context/Example (EN): A case definition or usage may also have one or more actor parameters that represent roles played by an entity external to the subject of the case but necessary to the specification of the case.

Definition (EN): A case definition or usage is declared as a kind of calculation definition or usage (see 7.19.2 ), using the kind keyword case.
Context/Example (EN): use case def UseCaseDef1 { subject s1:Subject1; objective { doc /* This is the objective description. */ require requirement1; } }

Definition (EN): Instead, the keyword subject is used to identify the declaration of a subject parameter, which must come before the declaration of any other parameters in a case definition or usage.
Context/Example (EN): case def FaultRecovery { subject system : AutomationSystem; actor engineer : Person; objective { doc /* The engineer determines the cause of the system fault and resolves it returning the system to nominal operation. */ } }

Definition (EN): Similarly to the declaration of a subject parameter, these distinguished parameters are declared using the keyword actor rather than explicitly declaring their direction.
Context/Example (EN): case def FaultRecovery { subject system : AutomationSystem; actor engineer : Person; objective { doc /* The engineer determines the cause of the system fault and resolves it returning the system to nominal operation. */ } }

Equivalent (JP): ケースモデルライブラリ
Definition (EN): The base case definition and usage for are Case and cases from the Cases model library (see 9.2.15 ).
Context/Example (EN): The base case definition and usage for are Case and cases from the Cases model library (see 9.2.15 ).
